region_of_origin: USA (New York City); served in Singapore
food_subgroup: Premium fast-casual beef burger; single fresh Angus beef patty with American cheese, lettuce, tomato, and ShackSauce on a potato bun

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for the Shake Shack ShackBurger. FOODON_03315507 (hamburger) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for the ShackBurger. A0GBY (fast food beef burger) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for commercial fast-casual burger products.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard ShackBurger as served at Shake Shack Singapore, approximately 200g, providing approximately 520 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: ShackBurger — Shake Shack Singapore official nutritional data; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central composite beef burger entries for micronutrient verification
nutrient_source.organization: Shake Shack Singapore / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy 520 kcal, protein 22g, fat 28g, carbohydrate 40g, sodium 905mg per 200g burger) are derived from Shake Shack Singapore official nutritional disclosures, converted to per-100g basis. Micronutrients (potassium, calcium, iron, vitamins, zinc, selenium, phosphorus, magnesium) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite beef burger entries (USDA SR Legacy / Foundation Foods) and are consistent with the known ingredient composition (80/20 Angus beef, potato bun, American cheese, lettuce, tomato, mayonnaise-based sauce). Per-100g values: energy 260 kcal, protein 11g, fat 14g, carbohydrate 20g, sodium 452.5mg.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — potato bun and composite beef burger analogous estimate; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Shake Shack ShackBurger. GI ~62 is estimated from the potato bun component (GI ~60–65 per University of Sydney GI Database for potato-based bread products) combined with the fat and protein content of the beef patty and cheese, which moderates overall glucose response. This is consistent with the GI range for composite beef burgers on white/potato buns documented in peer-reviewed GI literature. GL per serving = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (62 × 40) / 100 = 24.8.

hypertension.key_factor: 905mg sodium per burger (452.5mg per 100g) — approximately 45% of the WHO daily sodium limit of 2,000mg in a single serving
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should treat the ShackBurger as an occasional food only; the combined sodium from American cheese (~280mg), ShackSauce (~120mg), beef patty, and potato bun delivers 905mg sodium per burger, clinically equivalent to a McDonald's Big Mac (approximately 880mg sodium), and regular consumption will significantly impair blood pressure control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~62 (medium), GL ~24.8 per burger — moderate glycaemic impact partially attenuated by 14g fat and 11g protein per 100g
type2_diabetes.guidance: For patients with Type 2 Diabetes, the ShackBurger carries a moderate glycaemic load of 24.8 per serving; the high fat content (28g per burger, 13g saturated) slows gastric emptying and blunts the glucose spike from the potato bun, but the overall carbohydrate load (40g per burger) and saturated fat burden require portion awareness and should be counted within the daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: 13g saturated fat per burger (6.5g per 100g) from 80/20 Angus beef blend and American cheese, plus 905mg sodium and 0.8g natural trans fat (CLA/vaccenic acid) per burger
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of the ShackBurger due to its high saturated fat content (13g per burger, approximately 65% of the AHA daily limit of 20g), high sodium (905mg), and cholesterol load (120mg per burger); while the natural beef trans fats (CLA and vaccenic acid) are less atherogenic than industrial trans fats, the overall lipid and sodium profile makes this a high-concern food for CVD management.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (13g per burger) from 80/20 beef blend and American cheese; natural CLA from beef tallow is mildly anti-inflammatory; zinc ~5mg per burger from fresh beef
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: For patients with rheumatoid arthritis, the ShackBurger's high saturated fat content (13g per burger) may promote systemic inflammation with frequent consumption, though the natural CLA from fresh beef tallow is less pro-inflammatory than industrial trans fats, and the zinc content (~5mg per burger) provides a genuinely anti-inflammatory micronutrient; occasional consumption is acceptable but high sodium (905mg) warrants caution.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Shake Shack ShackBurger (Singapore) is a premium fast-casual beef burger served at Shake Shack outlets in Singapore (Jewel Changi Airport, Orchard Road ION, VivoCity). It consists of a single fresh (never frozen) 80/20 Angus beef patty, grilled to order, topped with American cheese, crisp lettuce, ripe tomato, and the proprietary ShackSauce (a mayonnaise-based sauce with a tangy, slightly sweet flavour profile), all served on a soft potato bun. The potato bun has a slightly lower glycaemic index (~60–65) than a standard sesame seed bun. Per burger (~200g): approximately 520 kcal, 22g protein, 28g fat (13g saturated), 40g carbohydrate, GI ~62, GL ~24.8, and 905mg sodium. Per 100g: 260 kcal, 11g protein, 14g fat, 6.5g saturated fat, 20g carbohydrate, 4.5g sugar, 0.5g dietary fibre, 452.5mg sodium. From a clinical perspective, the ShackBurger is nutritionally similar to a McDonald's Big Mac (880mg sodium vs 905mg sodium) despite its premium positioning and fresh-beef credentials. The American cheese contributes approximately 280mg sodium and the ShackSauce approximately 120mg sodium per burger. The 80/20 beef blend provides the highest saturated fat content of any single-patty burger in this database. Natural beef tallow trans fats (CLA and vaccenic acid, ~0.8g per burger) are present at low levels and are less atherogenic than industrial trans fats. Zinc (~5mg per burger) from the fresh beef is genuinely anti-inflammatory. Singapore pricing: approximately S$11–13 per burger. Shake Shack was founded in 2004 in New York City and is positioned as a premium QSR ('fast casual') brand.
common_user_queries: Shake Shack vs McDonald's Big Mac nutrition

confidence_reason: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium) are sourced from Shake Shack Singapore official nutritional disclosures, which constitute a verified primary source for this commercial product. Micronutrients are cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central composite beef burger entries and are consistent with the known ingredient composition. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using the potato bun as the primary carbohydrate vehicle, with the estimation basis clearly documented.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for the ShackBurger is not reported in Shake Shack Singapore official nutritional disclosures, and no exact match exists in USDA FoodData Central for this specific product formulation; values are not available from approved sources as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in Shake Shack Singapore official nutritional disclosures or USDA FoodData Central for this composite product. trans_fat_g (0.4g per 100g, 0.8g per burger) represents natural beef tallow trans fats (CLA and vaccenic acid) from the 80/20 Angus beef blend; these are not industrial trans fats and are less atherogenic. GI ~62 is an analogous estimate based on the potato bun GI range (60–65) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for the ShackBurger exists in the published literature. Halal status: Shake Shack Singapore is not certified halal as of the data access date; is_halal is set to false. The input record contained a truncated data_source field and an incomplete food_subgroup field; these have been corrected in this output. The Chinese name used ('摇摇汉堡') is a phonetic/semantic approximation of 'Shake Shack' in Mandarin Chinese; no official Chinese-language brand name has been published by Shake Shack Singapore.
